methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $96M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Arbor Energy is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Brad Hartwig that has raised $96M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set comprising 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with primary focus on three same-sector, same-stage private peers: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $96M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying a $192M valuation. Peer-median analysis anchors to the median valuation of the three same-stage Climate & Energy comparables at $150M, with observed range of $110M to $200M. These methods triangulate to a median estimate of $171M, with a range spanning $128M (low) to $221M (high). The $43M spread between low and high reflects meaningful variance in peer multiples and stage-progression assumptions. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, unit economics maturity, and whether the peer set adequately captures Arbor's specific technology risk profile and market position within the broader climate tech landscape.
